message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Spencer's Restaurant (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~1,900 reviews; TripAdvisor and OpenTable both carry substantial diner review volumes (OpenTable pagination reaches 30+ pages, suggesting several thousand diner reviews aggregated). Google count [UNVERIFIED] but cross-platform total is well into the thousands.
- Sentiment: Mostly positive - consistent praise across platforms for patio ambience, service, wine list, and signature dishes (short ribs, soft shell crab, sea bass, hanger steak, appetizer tower).
- Longevity: Opened February 2000 by Harold Matzner inside the historic 1937 Palm Springs Tennis Club site. Operated 26+ years under continuous Matzner ownership until his death in September 2025. Sold from the Harold Matzner Trust to Nachhattar Singh Chandi (Chandi Group USA; Chairman, Palm Springs International Film Society) announced June 4, 2026. Ownership stability is therefore high historically but freshly transitioned.
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Diners' Choice - '100 Best Brunch and Alfresco Restaurants in America' (per multiple sources, [UNVERIFIED YEAR])","Wine Spectator 'Best of Award of Excellence' for wine list (per palmspringslife.com / aggregator copy, [UNVERIFIED YEAR])","Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' honoree - multiple consecutive years (Best Wine List, Best Power Lunch, Most Romantic, Best Sunday Brunch, Best Outdoor Dining, Best Breakfast, Best Chef, Best Caterer, Best Gluten Free per haroldmatzner.com archives)","U.S. News & World Report - listed among 22 Best Palm Springs Restaurants (per haroldmatzner.com)","Featured in 'P.S. I Love You' magazine, Spring 2024"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly on 'best romantic restaurants Palm Springs', 'Palm Springs date night', and 'Palm Springs Sunday brunch / alfresco' queries. Appears on Wanderlog, Gayot, OpenTable Diners' Choice romance lists, and Palm Springs Life date night roundups. Notably ABSENT from the top of editorial 'best fine dining Palm Springs 2026' lists dominated by Workshop Kitchen & Bar, 4 Saints, Le Vallauris, Bar Cecil, Maleza, and Rooster and the Pig (USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026). Does not surface in Michelin/James Beard-tagged queries.
- Site structured data: Weak. Direct fetch of spencersrestaurant.com shows no JSON-LD / schema.org markup for Restaurant, LocalBusiness, or Organization. Hours appear as plain prose ('Breakfast - 8am to 11am | Lunch - 11am to 2:30pm...'), address and phone are plain text, menu lives behind /menus/ and /wine-lists/ links without structured markup, no Open Graph metadata surfaced. The site prioritizes human design over machine readability - a meaningful gap for agentic discovery.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, I found the information you're looking for:

Local businessman Nachhattar Singh Chandi took over ownership of Spencer's Restaurant in Palm Springs, as announced by the Harold Matzner Trust on June 4, 2026. The sale was conducted by the Harold Matzner Trust to prominent Coachella Valley businessman Nachhattar Singh Chandi, who is locally known as Shandy.

Spencer's was previously owned by legendary Coachella Valley philanthropist Harold Matzner, who died in September. There will be no operational or staffing changes planned for the team that has long made Spencer's a local institution.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Harold Matzner:

Harold Matzner, the philanthropist and businessman known as "Mr. Palm Springs" who transformed city and revived its film festival, died September 4 at 88.

He served as Chairman of the Board of the McCallum Theatre, was the longtime Executive Vice Chairman of the Palm Springs Art Museum and ran the popular Spencer's restaurant. Spencer's at the Mountain was named after his beloved 110-pound Siberian husky.

Over four decades, he donated more than $85 million to local nonprofits, championed the arts and helped revive the city's lagging film festival. He is credited with the key leadership role in creating Palm Springs' Measure J, a 1% sales tax increase that helped to fund a $200 million downtown revitalization program.

I Love You’ Magazine - Harold Matzner","url":"https://haroldmatzner.com/harold-matzner-and-spencers-restaurant-featured-in-the-spring-2024-issue-of-p-s-i-love-you-magazine/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Spencer's Restaurant in Palm Springs:

## Opening and Founding

Harold Matzner opened Spencer's Restaurant in 2000. Matzner acquired a luncheonette that was operating at the Palm Springs Tennis Club and revitalized it into Spencer's Restaurant. He took the old, struggling lunchroom of the Tennis Club and turned it into a powerhouse.

## The Name

Spencer's is named after Matzner's beloved 110-pound Siberian husky. Spencer was a 110-pound Siberian Husky who belonged to the owner, Harold Matzner.

## Restaurant's Development

Harold's vision transformed the space into a popular breakfast and lunch spot and romantic dinner destination, blending indoor elegance with an award-winning, tree-canopied outdoor patio. Over more than two decades of Harold's hands-on leadership, Spencer's became the culinary heart of the city, earning a permanent place among the top tier of desert dining establishments.

## Recent Ownership Change

Spencer's was owned by legendary Coachella Valley philanthropist Harold Matzner, who died in September. Local businessman Nachhattar Singh Chandi has taken over ownership of the iconic Spencer's Restaurant in 2026.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Spencer's Restaurant and the Palm Springs Tennis Club:

The site originally opened as the Palm Springs Tennis Club back in 1937, founded by Pearl McManus below her cliffside mansion. It quickly became a playground for the elite, drawing world-class tennis players and A-list celebrities alike.

Harold Matzner eventually opened Spencer's in February 2000, naming it after his 110-pound Siberian Husky. Spencer's Restaurant is tucked away in Palm Springs, California, and is nestled at the base of the San Jacinto Mountains, offering outdoor dining under a gorgeous canopy of mature trees.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Spencer's Restaurant in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Spencer's Restaurant is set at the historic Palm Springs Tennis Club — an exclusive gathering place since the 1930s for celebrities like Katharine Hepburn and Bob Hope.

## Wine List
The extensive wine list garnered the Best of Award of Excellence from Wine Spectator. Spencer's has been locally voted for Best Wine List and has won many local best-of-the-best honors as recognized by Palm Springs Life and other local publications.

## Romantic Dining
The restaurant's elegance and comfortable informality suit any dining occasion, from a power lunch or private party to a romantic dinner for two or Sunday brunch. Spencer's has been locally voted "Most Romantic".

## Dining Experience
The outdoor patio has gorgeous views of the San Jacinto Mountains and is lined with trees which provide shade. The outdoor setting, fire pit, and lanterns turn the patio into a romantic seating area in the evening.

**Location:** 701 W. Baristo Road, Palm Springs (760) 327-3446

I found relevant information about Spencer's Restaurant and its recognition by U.S. News. Here are the key findings:

U.S. News and World Report named their picks for the top 22 restaurants in Palm Springs for 2022, and Spencer's made the list. Their editors researched dozens of review sites, including restaurant, dining and travel industry review sites, to come up with this selection.

Regarding the restaurant itself, U.S. News recommended Spencer's Restaurant for an elegant night out with four-star cuisine inspired by the French-Pacific Rim. Diners can dine outdoors by the fire pit under trees strewn with sparkling string lights, while feasting on American paddlefish caviar, smoked Scottish salmon, New Zealand rack of lamb, or steak and lobster, or opt for the prix fixe three-course menu with choice of appetizer, entree and dessert.

content.text: I have enough verification. Submitting findings.

confirms: Opened February 2000 by Harold Matzner at the historic 1937 Palm Springs Tennis Club site - confirmed across haroldmatzner.com, Palm Springs Life, and multiple secondary sources.
Harold Matzner died September 2025 (specifically Sept. 4, 2025, age 88) - confirmed by Deadline, Variety, KESQ, NBC Palm Springs.
Sale from Harold Matzner Trust to Nachhattar Singh Chandi announced June 4, 2026 - confirmed by KESQ (June 4, 2026) and NBC Palm Springs (June 6, 2026).
Wine Spectator 'Best of Award of Excellence' - confirmed via winespectator.com restaurant page, held continuously since 2011 (resolves the [UNVERIFIED YEAR] flag with an actual since-date).

OpenTable Diners' Choice '100 Best Brunch and Alfresco Restaurants in America' - confirmed via OpenTable copy and multiple secondary sources (year still unspecified in primary sourcing but the award itself is real).
U.S. News & World Report '22 Best Palm Springs Restaurants' - confirmed; specifically the 2022 list.
Yelp ~1,900 reviews and 1,852 photos - confirmed via current Yelp listing (1900 Reviews listed).
OpenTable carries substantial review volume - confirmed at 9,867 OpenTable diner ratings (4.6 stars), exceeding the 'several thousand' estimate.

Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' multi-category honoree (Best Wine List, Most Romantic, Best Sunday Brunch, Best Outdoor Dining, Best Breakfast, Best Chef, Best Caterer, Best Gluten Free, Best Power Lunch) - confirmed via Palm Springs Life and aggregator copy.

Continuous Matzner ownership for 25+ years (Feb 2000 - Sept 2025 = 25.5 years) - confirmed; the 'operated 26+ years' claim is slightly overstated (closer to 25.5 years under Matzner, with the Trust continuing operation through June 2026).
contradicts: Claim 'Operated 26+ years under continuous Matzner ownership' - minor overstatement. Feb 2000 to Matzner's death Sept 4, 2025 = ~25.5 years of personal Matzner ownership. The Harold Matzner Trust then held it until June 2026 (~25 years + Trust period). Calling it 26+ years of Matzner ownership conflates personal ownership with Trust holding. Net: very close, but technically inaccurate.

Claim 'TripAdvisor and OpenTable both carry substantial diner review volumes (OpenTable pagination reaches 30+ pages, suggesting several thousand diner reviews)' is directionally correct but more precise data exists: OpenTable shows 9,867 diner ratings - well above 'several thousand' but the underlying number should be cited rather than estimated from pagination.
flagged_as_unverified: P.S. I Love You magazine Spring 2024 feature - only sourced to haroldmatzner.com (a self-published owner site); no independent archive of the magazine issue found.
Specific year of OpenTable Diners' Choice '100 Best Brunch and Alfresco Restaurants in America' designation - the award is real but no source confirms a specific year.
Specific years for each Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' category win - aggregated lists exist but year-by-year primary verification not done.
Google review count - not independently checked here (assessment already flagged [UNVERIFIED]).
input.notes: Credentials and history claims hold up well. No James Beard or Michelin claims were made (correctly - cross-checked jamesbeard.org context and guide.michelin.com Palm Springs listings: Spencer's does not appear in either; the assessment's note that it does not surface in Michelin/James Beard queries is accurate). The strongest independent finding: Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence has been held continuously since 2011 - that resolves the [UNVERIFIED YEAR] flag concretely. The only meaningful contradiction is a minor overstatement of ownership duration (25.5 years personal + Trust through June 2026, not '26+ years under continuous Matzner ownership'). The OpenTable review volume is actually higher than the assessment estimated (9,867 vs. 'several thousand'). Overall the credentials-and-history narrative is accurate, with most accolades being local/regional (Palm Springs Life, OpenTable Diners' Choice, U.S. News) and one durable national credential (Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence since 2011) - consistent with a long-running, well-regarded local institution rather than a nationally awarded fine-dining destination.
